exampleSentencesTranslated: I always keep my bedroom clean every day.
This restaurant is very clean so I like to eat here.
Remember to wash your hands clean before eating.
notes_md:
**sạch sẽ** means clean, neat, not dirty. It’s about hygiene and tidiness.

It’s an adjective, so it describes things like rooms, clothes, streets, hands, places, etc. In Vietnamese, adjectives usually come **after** the noun (nhà sạch sẽ) and can also stand alone as the main idea (nhà này sạch sẽ).

Compared to just **sạch** (clean), **sạch sẽ** sounds a bit softer and more about overall neatness. Opposite: **bẩn** (dirty).
notes:
sạch sẽ means clean, neat, not dirty. It’s about hygiene and tidiness.

It’s an adjective, so it describes things like rooms, clothes, streets, hands, places, etc. In Vietnamese, adjectives usually come after the noun (nhà sạch sẽ) and can also stand alone as the main idea (nhà này sạch sẽ).

Compared to just sạch (clean), sạch sẽ sounds a bit softer and more about overall neatness. Opposite: bẩn (dirty).
exampleSentences: Em luôn giữ phòng ngủ của mình sạch sẽ mỗi ngày.
Nhà hàng này rất sạch sẽ nên anh thích ăn ở đây.
Chị nhớ rửa tay sạch sẽ trước khi ăn cơm nhé.
